master_claim_table.claim: Every method ranks the same 101 candidates per user under the same score schema, importer, metrics, provenance checks, and paired tests.
master_claim_table.evidence: paper-facing ledger, same-candidate gates, and evidence packaging audits

master_claim_table.claim: All methods use Qwen3-8B whenever an LLM or LLM-derived representation is required.
master_claim_table.evidence: official baseline provenance and fairness policy

master_claim_table.claim: C-CRP defines a calibrated candidate posterior with uncertainty components and a validation-controlled ranking family.
master_claim_table.evidence: Method equations, signal provenance, selector grid, and fail-closed gates

master_claim_table.claim: C-CRP ranks first against eight official-code-level LLM recommendation baselines on HR@5/@10/@20, NDCG@5/@10/@20, and MRR across Sports, Toys, Home, and Tools.
master_claim_table.evidence: cross-domain certificate, full metric ledger, main results table, and rank-by-metric table

master_claim_table.claim: All 56 per-domain C-CRP-vs-baseline paired tests are positive and Holm-significant in each domain.
master_claim_table.evidence: paired-test summary using 2,000 paired event bootstrap samples and Holm alpha 0.05

master_claim_table.claim: C-CRP event-level uncertainty stratifies ranking reliability in all four domains.
master_claim_table.evidence: observation_four_domain provenance and uncertainty stratification table

master_claim_table.claim: Component ablations reveal several uncertainty terms are weak, neutral, or redundant rather than uniformly necessary.
Validation-selected eta and uncertainty-weight settings are stable within the pre-registered NDCG@10 tolerance.
A positive risk penalty is not necessary or uniformly beneficial in current sweeps.
master_claim_table.evidence: eta test-best zero in all four domains and risk-penalty ablation

master_claim_table.claim: The result is scoped to controlled same-candidate reranking, not full-catalog recommender SOTA.
master_claim_table.evidence: protocol definition and explicit exclusion of candidate generation/full-catalog retrieval

master_claim_table.claim: Official-code-level baselines preserve their declared mechanisms and use official/default or recommended hyperparameters under the unified score contract.
master_claim_table.evidence: baseline_provenance_summary table, official baseline configs, and local evidence ledger

master_claim_table.claim: Current limitations include LLM cost/latency, prompt/parser dependence, sampled-negative candidate sets, external adapter policy, and no full-catalog claim.

red_flag_scan.significantly: Appears only in Holm-significant paired-test context and is backed by paired tests.
red_flag_scan.SOTA: Appears only as a negated full-catalog SOTA boundary.
red_flag_scan.first: No first/novel priority claim is used.
red_flag_scan.necessary: Positive necessity claims are explicitly negated for components/risk penalty.
red_flag_scan.robust: The text uses stability/sensitivity for hyperparameters rather than broad robustness.

minor_issues: Remaining small overfull hbox warnings are layout polish, not claim blockers.
ProEx and ProMax proceedings metadata should be rechecked immediately before final submission.
remaining_process_blockers: Run another section-level top-conference review on the latest draft.
